title: Cyrillic homoglyph bypass also defeats ignore/you-are strip patterns
Test at session-resume-auth.vitest.ts:212-220 locks in R1-P1-002 no-propagation behaviour as intentional
hooks-shared-provenance.vitest.ts only tests Greek homoglyph, Cyrillic/Latin cases never regression-locked
Root vitest.config.ts vitest/config import breaks when vitest is invoked from scripts/ cwd

reason: iter-6 refuted attacker _extra.sessionId forgery (MCP SDK sources from transport). Under stdio guard is vacuous but there is no cross-trust boundary (single-UID subprocess). Under HTTP guard functions correctly. Compound claim conflates guard-vacuity with guard-bypass.

claim: NFKC is the root cause of homoglyph bypass
note: NFKC handles full-width/Greek; the real gap is the explicit Greek-only fold table in normalizeRecoveredPayloadLineForMatching

rationale: no new P0/P1 code findings; remaining items are test-coverage + infrastructure gaps. Iter-5 P0 retracted via iter-6 + iter-7.
